detail_desc:
The guardsman looks at you with a mixture of curiosity and mild animosity.
Seeing that you wear no colors for any of the Barons, he is not sure what to
make of you.
long_desc: A guardsman stands at ease near the barrier, casually watching your arrival.

detail_desc:
The man must have traveled some distance to get the brew that only Westlawn
can make so well.  He carries the barrels, one on each shoulder, a bead of
sweat running down the bridge of his nose.
long_desc: A tradesman laden down with barrels of Westlawn's finest trudges past.

detail_desc:
The road being so rutted and in poor shape, it requires constant attention
from the workers of the area.  The serf carts dirt from dry areas onto the
road, trying to dry up the areas of the road that are muddiest.
long_desc: A man with a crude wheel barrow works on the road.

detail_desc:
He smiles up at you with a beguiling twinkle in his eye.  How could he know
that his life is less than perfect with such a fine pile of mud right outside
his front door?
long_desc: A little boy covered in mud and filth plays with a pile of mud.

detail_desc:
The poor dear does not wish to step off the doorstep because she knows she
will instantly be a mess from the muddy yard, but she does not wish to stay
indoors all day.
long_desc: A little girl stands forlornly at the door to her family's hut.

detail_desc:
He is filthy, covered in dirt and grime that seems almost a pigment to his
skin rather than a covering of extra mess.
long_desc: A man makes his way home after a long day's work.

detail_desc:
Her eyes perpetually squint from the sun, her face pinched and leathery as
she watches the children at play.
long_desc: An old woman with a wrinkled and careworn face stands outside her hovel.

detail_desc:
He is a dirty, filthy child - a child of the dregs, who's lot it is in life
to do nothing but labor for others.
long_desc: A young child plays outside his home under the watchful eyes of his mother.

detail_desc:
Gilsby was in the service of the old Baron, Westlawn's father for many years
before Westlawn took on the mantle of Baron.  Gilsby has seen them come and
seen them go and holds within his old head many secrets to the keep and the
surrounding area.
long_desc: An old man stands behind the bar slowly and deliberately serving drinks and food.

detail_desc:
He sits at a chair at a table, both elbows propped up on the table, slumped
over with all his weight on those elbows.
long_desc: A tired-looking man leans on a table, tiredly sipping at his ale.

detail_desc:
It is his eyes, those secretive eyes, that give you pause when you look his
way.  He is certainly not one of the serfs of the area, and does not wear the
red of Baron Westlawn.
long_desc: A man leans against the far side of the bar, his eyes trained upon you.

detail_desc:
Her wrinkled hands move with strength and fluidity which belies the image
she presents upon first look.
long_desc: A woman sits upon a barrel working the leather of a harness.

detail_desc:
He climbs high up into the heights of the hay stacks, looking down every
once in a while to assure himself that he is truly ascending.
long_desc: A little boy plays on the stacks of hay, laughing in delight.

detail_desc:
She nonchalantly climbs the stacks after the boy, trying to make it appear
that all of this hay climbing is foolish and beneath her, but she never takes
her eyes of the boy and there is a slight smile at the corners of her mouth.
long_desc: A little girl coquettishly tries to act as if she isn't chasing the boy.

detail_desc: Its tail swishes back and forth, lazily swatting the flies that buzz about.
long_desc: An old cow with sagging udders lazily wanders the field.

detail_desc: Her back is bowed in the center, her coat patched and in poor condition.
long_desc: An old nag slowly makes its way around the area, looking for fresh grass.

detail_desc:
He idly dangles his legs over the side, swinging them lazily as the day
passes.  It doesn't appear that he really cares whether or not he catches
anything, he is just enjoying the quiet.
long_desc: One of the locals sits on the bridge's edge with a fishing pole.

detail_desc:
She walks slightly bent over to one side, the contents of the basket
obviously heavy and awkward.
long_desc: A woman hoists a large wicker basket on one shoulder, carrying it through the yard.

detail_desc:
This man is one of Westlawn's many servants whose main job it is to make
sure that the dining and kitchen area is always well-stocked with ale.
long_desc: A man pushes a wooden keg through the keep, taking it to another room.

detail_desc:
His keen eyes do not for one moment waver from their incessant scanning of
the lands around the keep.  If this man fails in his job, many people could
die.
long_desc: A soldier stands at post, scanning the horizon for any signs of invasion.

detail_desc:
All of Westlawn's men have been trained so well that they continue to march
in formation even when there is no formation.
long_desc: A soldier tromps about the keep, stepping in clean rhythm even though he is alone.

detail_desc:
Westlawn's Elite are feared throughout the realm by all Barons and common
folk alike.  They have been trained in the arts of battle to such a degree as
to be masters, respected as the best in nearly every fight.
long_desc: A soldier wearing the sigil of an Elite strides past.

detail_desc:
Jerrod smiles and nods as he passes by, his face set in an expression of
open kindness.  The people who he smiles at, however, smile nervously back,
hoping not to offend in any way.  Jerrod is never petty or unkind to the common
folk of the keep or the surrounding land, but the stories of his tirades on his
men are legendary - thus the reason for the wide berth that people give when he
passes.
long_desc: People stand aside as Jerrod, the captain of the Elite, makes his way through.

detail_desc:
He is nondescript in nearly every way possible.  His clothes are those of a
common man's - brown jerkin with a pair of sandals.
long_desc: A raggedy commoner walks past.

detail_desc:
He is probably the only man in the immediate area who is not covered in mud
and filth from head to toe.
long_desc: The foreman of the farm crew stands scribbling in a clipboard.

detail_desc:
He is tall and lean, well muscled but not huge like many stereotypical smiths
of the area.  He looks up at you without stopping his pounding, winks with a sly
smile, and goes back to concentrating on his anvil.
long_desc: Donld the smithy pounds away at his anvil.

detail_desc:
He is not a particularly large or imposing man, he is simply a man.  He is
better than average looking, about six foot even in height and carries himself
with a strong dignity.
long_desc: The Baron Westlawn is here, talking with Donld.

detail_desc:
She is dressed in a very fetching homespun dress that amplifies her bosoms
to the full extent.  Her hair is done up high in a nest of sorts, stray wisps
falling about her face.
long_desc: A serving wench moves about the keep, performing her duties.

detail_desc:
He is dressed in a white kitchen-workers outfit, stained and nastified by
the many juices and fluids of the food he prepares.
long_desc: A cook hustles about the kitchen, staying clear of Salida's spoon.

detail_desc:
She is up to her elbows in dirty dish water, her face covered in a sheen of
sweat and grime.  But she is smiling.  Who can understand women?
long_desc: A cook works feverishly at scrubbing the pots and pans for the next meal.

detail_desc:
Anyone who has frequented the kitchen at all knows to stay clear of this big
woman's spoon and the lashings it will endow.  Just the same, she runs a great
kitchen with great food.
long_desc: Salida booms orders in her huge voice, her large wooden spoon held at ready.

detail_desc:
From the looks of it, this nasty creature couldn't be happier as it rolls
and twists about in the mud.
long_desc: A stray pig runs about the yard, snorting and oinking.

detail_desc:
He looks up at you pitifully with pain-filled eyes, silently beseeching that
you let him alone.
long_desc: An old man, one of the original serfs of the area, lounges on the floor.

detail_desc:
He is a younger lad - possibly fourteen or fifteen years of age - who wishes
to make a go at becoming one of the Elite.  All of the younger generation here
at Warrior's Keep have that same desire - to one day be a proud warrior who
fights at the Baron's side.
long_desc: One of Westlawn's servants tends to the horses.

detail_desc:
He chews a piece of straw slowly, deliberately, as he watches one of the
newest of the steeds trot about the field.
long_desc: Roscoe, the chief stablemaster and horsebreaker, leans on the fence.

detail_desc:
He stares up at the ceiling, not really paying any attention to anything at
all.
long_desc: A soldier lounges on a cot, taking a break before he goes on watch.

detail_desc:
She somehow stays just of reach from Salida's spoon, winking slyly and
dodging around piles of pots and pans.
long_desc: A mischievous child darts through the kitchen, stealing any scraps she can find.

detail_desc:
He has a look of confidentiality about him - as if what he whispers is of
great import and groundbreaking news.
long_desc: A noble whispers quietly to the others gathered around him.

detail_desc:
The young man looks quite open and trusting - perhaps too trusting.  He
listens raptly, nodding every now and again to what is being said.
long_desc: A young noble listens as the secretive one whispers.

detail_desc:
He frequently rolls his eyes, looking for all the world as if he couldn't
disagree more or be more bored - but he doesn't leave.
long_desc: A haughty noble listens to it all with an air of disdain.

detail_desc:
She holds her head defiantly, listening only slightly to what the noble
whispers, worried more about her status being called down.
long_desc: A noblewoman stands boldly with the men, taking her chances at being laughed out.

detail_desc:
He carries most of what he owns in a bag slung over one shoulder - the rest
must be stashed away somewhere in one of the guest rooms.
long_desc: A visiting merchant walks through the yard, enjoying some time to himself.

detail_desc:
Some people carry pride in themselves at being very fast, some are proud of
their ability to fight, others love the adventure of learning new spells.  Bud
is proud of one thing only - the man loves his beer.  Crisp and clean, always
cold-filtered, never heat pasteurized.
long_desc: Bud, the alemaster of the keep, brews his finest.
